description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the image registry.gitlab.com/rilis/docker-ci:ci-latest with three stages: test, stats, and docs. In the test stage add a test job that runs hell --install -r "=" -r "RILI_SERVICE_SOCKET_TESTS=ON" -i "CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release" -i "CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=11" -G "Ninja", creates a build directory, runs cmake with Ninja and the same flags, executes ninja install, and finally runs ./rili-service-socket_tests. In the stats stage add four jobs: clang-format runs a find command piping *.hpp and *.cpp files to clang-format -i -style=file, records git status to ../status.log, shows git diff and fails if the log is non‑empty; coverage runs hell --install with test and coverage flags, builds with cmake and ninja, runs the test binary, captures coverage with lcov, filters out system and test paths, generates HTML with genhtml, lists coverage, and publishes the coverage directory as an artifact named $CI_JOB_NAME that expires in 1 day; cpplint runs a find command piping *.cpp and *.hpp files to cpplint.py with specific linelength and filter options; valgrind runs a similar build in Debug mode and then executes valgrind --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=all --error-exitcode=123 on the test binary. In the docs stage add a doxygen job that runs doxygen Doxyfile and stores the generated doxygen/html directory as an artifact that expires in 1 week. No explicit rules, caches, or extra variables are defined.
